opp.name: VanCity S2P RFI
opp.next_step: 6/3 - ROM pricing estimate presented 5/28MF - asked for pricing, commercial inputs document sent to Laetitia and team 5/25MF - RFI response submitted 4/14MF RFI received, SC resource assigned (Srishti). Fitment analysis has been completed. Questions submitted 4/22, waiting for responses by 4/29 SC fitment assessment - I’ve completed an initial review of the Vancity requirement set and wanted to share a quick perspective from a fitment standpoint. The scope spans the full Source-to-Pay lifecycle, covering intake management strategic sourcing, supplier performance and risk management, contract lifecycle management, procurement and purchasing, invoicing, as well as reporting and analytics. Overall, this reflects a comprehensive, end-to-end S2P scope. The only area that I saw as a snag would be automated redlining but this of course is quite a minor gap and can be solutioned. Based on this assessment, the alignment appears strong across the functional areas in scope, both in terms of capability coverage and overall solution positioning. Issued 4/14 Intent to respond due 4/20 - complete Questions due 4/22 - submitted Responses expected 4/29 Response due 5/26 Presentations will take place between 6/15-6/18
opp.ais_why:
• April RFI covers intake, strategic sourcing, supplier performance and risk, and CLM — intake is in scope but no explicit Merlin or agentic language from the customer.
• The only functional snag flagged is automated redlining (a CLM gap), not an AI capability — the signal is RFI-driven, not AI-led excitement.
• RFI received 4/14, response due 5/26, and presentations 6/15-6/18 — active cadence but customer-voice AI cues are missing.

items.quote: The only area that I saw as a snag would be automated redlining but this of course is quite a minor gap and can be solutioned.

sources: SF Next Step field (scope description, no metrics)
SF custom fields query (no value/metrics fields present in org)
Avoma discovery (zero buyer calls matched)
metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem, ROI case, or financial impact documented anywhere. The Next Step field describes comprehensive S2P scope (intake, sourcing, supplier risk, CLM, procurement, invoicing, analytics) but carries zero financial metrics, cost-savings target, headcount reduction, cycle-time improvement, or error-rate reduction. No Salesforce fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c) exist or are populated in this org to anchor a value case. No Avoma calls discovered to extract buyer-stated pain or ROI expectations. Without quantified value, the deal is vulnerable to deprioritization, budget cuts, or a do-nothing outcome. The scope is broad (seven products) but the business case is invisible, which is fatal for a $250k enterprise S2P investment requiring C-level sign-off.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (zero records)
SF Next Step (Laetitia named, no role/advocacy evidence)
SF custom fields query (no champion fields present)
Avoma discovery (zero buyer calls)
Engagement pulse (18 days silent, single-threaded risk)
champion.narrative: No champion identified or developed. Zero contact roles on the opportunity, so no Champion role assigned. The only named buyer contact is 'Laetitia' (mentioned in Next Step as recipient of pricing documents 28 May and 03 June), but her title, role, authority, and advocacy are completely unknown—she may be a procurement coordinator, not a champion. No evidence of Laetitia (or any buyer contact) providing internal intel, driving Zycus's agenda, introducing the Economic Buyer or Decision Maker, or pushing back on competitors. No Salesforce fields (X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Decision_Maker_Identified__c) exist in this org. No Avoma calls to assess champion development or engagement depth. The deal is single-threaded into a procurement contact with no multi-threading, no executive access, and no advocate. This is a critical gap 81 days from close in a competitive RFI: if Laetitia is the sole thread and she has gone quiet for 18 days post-presentation, the deal is at high risk of stalling or being lost silently.

sources: SF Competitors__c (empty)
SF custom fields query (no competitor fields present)
SF Next Step (multi-vendor RFI confirmed, no names)
Avoma discovery (zero buyer calls)
competition.narrative: Competitive landscape completely dark. The Salesforce Competitors__c field is empty (ground truth: 'unknown'). No other competitor-related fields (Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, Moved_To__c, Why_not_Zycus__c) exist in this org to capture competitive intel. The Next Step confirms this is a multi-vendor RFI process (vendor presentations 15-18 June), so Zycus is competing against at least 2-4 other S2P platforms (likely Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Ivalua, or Jaggaer given the enterprise scope and geography), but zero competitor names, no intel on shortlist ranking, no feedback on how Zycus's presentation landed versus rivals, and no visibility into pricing or feature comparison. No Avoma calls to extract competitive mentions. The 18-day silence post-presentations could mean Zycus is shortlisted and the buyer is deliberating, OR Zycus was eliminated and the buyer has moved on to finalists—completely unknown. Without competitive intel, Zycus is flying blind and cannot execute a wedge or counter-positioning strategy.

sources: SF Next Step (scope description, no pain articulation)
SF custom fields query (no pain fields present)
Avoma discovery (zero buyer calls)
identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain documented. The Next Step describes comprehensive S2P scope (intake, sourcing, supplier risk/performance, CLM, procurement, invoicing, analytics) as the functional requirement set, but carries zero articulation of the pain driving the RFI: no mention of manual sourcing bottlenecks, supplier risk exposure, contract leakage, maverick spend, invoice errors, compliance gaps, or ERP integration friction. No Salesforce fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Compelling_Event__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c) exist in this org to anchor pain. No Avoma calls to capture the buyer's pain narrative. The only pain clue is the minor gap Srishti flagged: automated redlining in CLM, which implies the buyer may be managing contract redlines manually today, but this is inferred, not stated by the buyer, and is labeled 'quite minor.' Without a named pain owner, quantified impact, or compelling event (budget year-end, compliance deadline, incumbent contract expiration), the deal lacks urgency and is vulnerable to延期 or cancellation.

sources: SF Next Step (ROM pricing, commercial inputs shared, no contract mechanics)
SF custom fields query (no paper-process fields present)
Avoma discovery (zero buyer calls)
paper_process.narrative: No contracting, legal, procurement, or InfoSec process visibility. No Salesforce fields exist in this org to capture MSA/SOW mechanics, legal review, procurement channel, or security review. The Next Step mentions 'commercial inputs document sent to Laetitia and team' (28 May) and 'ROM pricing estimate presented' (03 June), implying Zycus has shared pricing structure, but no buyer feedback on contract terms, redlining expectations (beyond the minor automated-redlining gap flagged in fitment), or procurement workflow (direct buy vs three-way PO vs partner-led). No SI or partner role mentioned. No Current_Contract_Expiration__c field present to indicate an incumbent replacement forcing function. For a $250k, seven-product S2P deal, paper process likely includes MSA negotiation, SOW scoping for implementation, InfoSec/data-privacy review, and ERP integration sign-off, none of which are visible. This gap is typical at RFI stage (paper comes post-shortlist), but the 18-day post-presentation silence with no paper-process kickoff is a stall risk.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (zero records)
SF Next Step (Laetitia named, role/authority unknown)
SF custom fields query (no EB fields present in org)
Avoma discovery (zero buyer calls matched)
economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer completely unidentified. Zero contact roles on the opportunity, so no EB role assigned. No Salesforce fields (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c) exist in this org to flag EB identification. The Next Step references 'Laetitia and team' receiving pricing documents (28 May, 03 June), but Laetitia's title, budget authority, and role are unknown—she may be procurement/sourcing staff, not the commercial decision-maker. For a $250k S2P investment spanning seven products, the EB is likely a CFO, CPO, or VP Finance, none of whom are visible. No Avoma calls to reveal who controlled commercial or pricing conversations. This is a single-threaded deal into a procurement contact with zero visibility into C-level sponsorship or budget approval, which is indefensible 81 days from close.

sources: SF Next Step (RFI timeline, presentation dates)
SF custom fields query (no decision-process fields present)
Avoma discovery (zero buyer calls)
Engagement pulse (18 days silent post-presentations, no next milestone)
decision_process.narrative: The RFI process timeline is documented in Next Step: RFI issued 14 April, intent to respond due 20 April (completed), clarification questions submitted 22 April with buyer responses expected 29 April, RFI response due 26 May (submitted 25 May), and vendor presentations scheduled 15-18 June 2026. This maps a formal, multi-vendor procurement process: RFI round → clarifications → written responses → shortlist → presentations → (expected) shortlist decision → commercials/negotiation → selection. The process is buyer-led and competitive, typical of enterprise S2P RFIs. However, the post-presentation process is completely dark: no shortlist timeline communicated, no next milestone (deeper demo, commercial negotiation, reference calls, or finalist workshop) visible, and no approval chain documented. No Salesforce fields (What_is_the_decision_process__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Multiple_approvals__c) exist in this org. No Avoma calls to reveal the internal approval steps or C-level gate. The front half of the process (RFI mechanics) is confirmed; the back half (shortlist → selection → contract) is a gap, and the 18-day silence post-presentations suggests either internal deliberation or Zycus elimination.

sources: SF Next Step (Srishti fitment assessment, RFI scope)
SF Products (seven-product scope matches RFI breadth)
Avoma discovery (zero buyer calls to extract live criteria)
decision_criteria.narrative: The RFI response (submitted 25 May) and fitment assessment (completed by SC resource Srishti, logged in Next Step) confirm the buyer's evaluation criteria span full S2P lifecycle: intake management, strategic sourcing, supplier performance and risk, contract lifecycle management (CLM), procurement/purchasing, invoicing, and reporting/analytics. Srishti's assessment notes 'strong alignment across functional areas in scope' with one minor gap: automated redlining in CLM, flagged as 'quite minor and can be solutioned.' This suggests the buyer's RFI scoring weights breadth of S2P coverage, CLM depth, and likely AI/automation capability (Merlin is in scope). However, the criteria are inferred from Zycus's internal fitment read and the RFI scope document—NOT from the buyer's own scoring rubric, weighted priorities, or must-have vs nice-to-have breakdowns. No buyer-stated criteria captured on calls (zero Avoma matches). Vendor presentations 15-18 June likely revealed live scoring feedback, but none is logged. Criteria are partially known (scope-driven) but the buyer's weighting, Zycus's score, and competitive ranking remain dark.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Merlin included in seven-product scope signals buyer interest in AI-enabled S2P (likely for sourcing intelligence, contract analytics, supplier risk prediction, or procurement automation), but no AIS fields populated and no buyer-stated AI requirements or appetite captured. AI fit is inferred from product scope, not confirmed by buyer articulation or AIS scoring.

items.action: Executive connect: the deal owner's manager calls Laetitia (or requests introduction to her manager/the RFI program lead) to surface shortlist decision, post-presentation feedback, and Zycus's competitive standing. Reframe the call as a C-level partnership discussion (not a sales check-in) and request a 15-20 minute shortlist debrief within 48-72 hours. Goal: break the 18-day silence, confirm Zycus is still in play, and map the Economic Buyer and next milestone.
items.trigger: 18 days silent post-presentation (vendor demos 15-18 June), no shortlist decision, no buyer reply to rep outreach 18 June, in a competitive multi-vendor RFI
items.expected_effect: Confirms whether Zycus is shortlisted or eliminated, surfaces competitive standing and next milestone (finalist workshop, commercial negotiation, or reference calls), and escalates the thread from procurement coordinator to decision-maker or RFI lead. If Zycus is eliminated, cuts losses immediately; if shortlisted, unblocks progression and re-engages the buyer with executive presence.

items.action: Deal team: map the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker via LinkedIn, Vancity org chart research, or asking Laetitia directly ('Who on your side will approve the $250k S2P investment and sign the contract?'). Target roles: CFO, CPO, VP Finance, VP Procurement, or Head of Digital Transformation. Once identified, request an executive sponsor call (Zycus C-level or the deal owner's manager) with the EB to present ROI business case and secure budget sponsorship.
items.trigger: Zero contact roles, no EB/DM identified 81 days from close in a $250k enterprise S2P deal requiring C-level budget approval
items.expected_effect: Identifies the commercial decision-maker and budget authority, enables EB-level business case presentation (quantified ROI, compelling event), and de-risks the single-threaded procurement relationship. Without EB engagement, the deal is indefensible for forecast progression or close.

items.action: Deal team: extract competitive intel by asking Laetitia (or the RFI lead, if escalated): 'How many vendors presented 15-18 June, how many are being shortlisted, and what are the key differentiators you're evaluating across the finalists?' If the buyer is unwilling to share, infer the competitive set (likely Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer) and run a competitive review with the SC team to sharpen the wedge (Merlin AI agents for sourcing/contract intelligence, iRisk for supplier performance, breadth across seven products). Prepare a one-page 'Why Zycus' positioning doc emphasizing AI differentiation and end-to-end S2P coverage, and send it to Laetitia as a post-presentation follow-up.
items.trigger: Zero competitor intel in a confirmed multi-vendor RFI, 18 days post-presentation with no shortlist feedback
items.expected_effect: Surfaces competitive standing, enables targeted counter-positioning against named rivals, and reinforces Zycus's differentiation (AI/Merlin, breadth, S2P depth) in the buyer's shortlist deliberation. Without competitive intel, Zycus cannot execute a wedge strategy and risks losing on price or feature comparison.

items.action: Deal team: build a quantified ROI business case with the buyer (ideally the Economic Buyer or a finance stakeholder, once mapped). Run a 30-45 minute ROI workshop to extract current-state pain (manual sourcing cycle time, contract leakage %, maverick spend $, invoice error rate, supplier risk exposure) and model savings/impact across the seven-product S2P scope (iSource cycle-time reduction, iContract leakage prevention, iRisk compliance cost avoidance, Merlin negotiation savings via AI agents). Deliver a one-page ROI summary (3-year NPV, payback period, cost-savings $ or %) and position it as the financial justification for the EB/CFO to approve the $250k investment.
items.trigger: No quantified business case, ROI target, or financial metrics documented; zero pain articulation or compelling event
items.expected_effect: Creates a financial justification for the $250k S2P investment, arms the EB/CFO with a defensible ROI case for budget approval, and differentiates Zycus from competitors who may not have quantified value. Without a quantified business case, the deal is vulnerable to deprioritization, budget cuts, or a do-nothing outcome, especially in a competitive RFI where buyers default to price comparison without a clear value anchor.

items.action: Deal team: multi-thread into at least two additional buyer stakeholders beyond Laetitia within 10 days. Target roles: sourcing lead (iSource user), contract manager (iContract user), supplier risk/quality owner (iRisk user), or finance/AP lead (invoicing user). Request 20-minute use-case validation calls per role to demonstrate product fit, extract role-specific pain, and build multiple advocates. Goal: de-risk the single-threaded procurement dependency and develop at least one champion with access to the Economic Buyer or Decision Maker.
items.trigger: Zero contact roles, single-threaded into Laetitia (procurement, role unknown), no champion, 81 days from close
items.expected_effect: Reduces single-thread risk (if Laetitia continues silent or is not the decision-maker, deal is not lost), builds multiple buyer relationships across S2P functional roles, and increases probability of identifying a champion with EB access or internal advocacy capability. Multi-threading is standard enterprise best practice and critical for a $250k, seven-product deal with no current champion.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is cooling and at high risk. Presentations completed 18 days ago with zero buyer feedback, no shortlist confirmation, and no next milestone. Single-threaded into a procurement contact who has gone quiet. No EB/DM/Champion mapped, no competitive intel, no quantified value case. Strong internal fitment assessment, but zero external buyer validation. The 18-day silence post-presentation in a multi-vendor RFI is a stall or silent-loss signal.

north_star_verdict.math: 81 days to close (2026-09-25). Deal created 15 April 2026, 82 days old. Time in Formal Evaluation: at least 82 days (no stage-date fields confirm earlier progression). RFI response submitted 25 May, ROM pricing presented 03 June, presentations 15-18 June. Last rep-initiated outreach 18 June per Next Step (awaiting buyer reply). No verified buyer touch since presentations ended 18 June, now 18 days silent. At current pace: 18 days of silence post-presentation in a competitive RFI is a stall signal. Required pace: shortlist decision and next milestone (commercial negotiation or deeper demo) needed within 7-10 days to stay on track for Q3 close. Forward slip risk: moderate; if shortlist delayed beyond mid-July, close slips to Q4. No submission-date fields populated to anchor true stage velocity.
evidence: Last rep touch 18 June (presentations window), no buyer reply in 18 days (SF Next Step)
Zero contact roles, no EB/DM/Champion mapped (SF OpportunityContactRole empty)
No competitor intel, no RFI bake-off visibility (SF Competitors__c empty, no call data)
ROM pricing presented 03 June, no buyer feedback on acceptance (SF Next Step)
Presentations 15-18 June passed with no logged outcome (SF Next Step, no tasks/events post-18 June)
Pipeline forecast with 81 days to close, single-threaded, EB unmapped, no champion (indefensible for Best Case/Commit)
north_star_verdict.headline: Competitive RFI gone quiet 18 days post-presentation with zero buyer feedback, no shortlist confirmation, and completely dark competitive intel. Single-threaded into procurement with no EB/DM mapped and no champion. Vendor demos completed 15-18 June; silence since then signals either internal deliberation or Zycus elimination.

flags: Re-engage the procurement team urgently to surface the outcome of the 15-18 June vendor presentations, confirm whether Zycus is on the shortlist, and secure the next milestone (deeper demo, finalist selection, or commercial negotiation). The deal has been silent for 8-10 days post-presentation, which is a stall signal in a competitive RFI process where other vendors may be advancing while Zycus waits.

Map and engage the Economic Buyer (likely CFO Frances Yip, VP Finance Kanupriya Sharma, or Director FP&A Shameel Mehdi) to validate budget approval, pricing acceptance, and executive sponsorship. Without EB engagement, the $250k forecast is speculative and the deal is vulnerable to a late-stage budget or approval stall.

Identify and develop a champion within the procurement or finance function who has access to the Economic Buyer, understands the internal decision process, and is willing to advocate for Zycus against competitors. The current single-threaded engagement (zero contact roles, no named champion) is the highest risk factor for this deal going dark or being lost to a competitor without Zycus knowing.

Single-threaded: zero contact roles, no multi-threading beyond Laetitia (procurement contact, role unknown). High risk of deal going dark if sole thread goes quiet (now happening: 18 days silent post-presentations).
Economic Buyer and Decision Maker unmapped: no EB/DM contact roles, no visibility into C-level sponsorship or budget approval for $250k S2P investment. Indefensible 81 days from close.
No champion: zero evidence of internal advocate, no contact providing intel or driving Zycus's agenda. Single procurement contact (Laetitia) with unknown advocacy.
Competitive intel dark: zero competitor names, no RFI shortlist ranking, no post-presentation feedback. Multi-vendor process confirmed, but Zycus is flying blind on competitive positioning.
18-day silence post-presentation: vendor demos 15-18 June, no buyer touch since (rep outreach 18 June awaiting reply). In a competitive RFI, this is a stall or elimination signal.
No quantified business case: zero financial metrics, ROI target, or compelling event documented. Vulnerable to deprioritization or do-nothing.
No post-presentation follow-up plan: presentations completed, but no next milestone (shortlist call, commercial deep-dive, reference customers, finalist workshop) visible or scheduled.
best_practice_check.summary: Critical gaps: single-threaded into procurement with no EB/DM/Champion mapped, zero competitive intel in a multi-vendor RFI, no quantified value case, and 18-day post-presentation silence with no shortlist confirmation. Deal is at high risk of silent loss.

competitive_position.summary: Competitive landscape completely dark in a confirmed multi-vendor RFI. Zero competitor names, no shortlist ranking, no post-presentation feedback, and no intel on how Zycus compares on pricing, features, or AI/Merlin positioning. The 18-day silence post-presentations (15-18 June) in a competitive process is a red flag: either the buyer is internally deliberating across all vendors, or Zycus has been eliminated and the buyer has moved to finalists without notification. Strong internal fitment assessment (Srishti's read: broad alignment, minor automated-redlining gap solutionable) but zero external validation from the buyer. No wedge strategy visible because competitive set is unknown.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin is in product scope (seven-product S2P suite includes Merlin), signaling AI appetite, but zero evidence of AI/Merlin positioning landing with the buyer, differentiating versus competitors, or being scored favorably in the RFI. No AIS fields populated. No Avoma calls to assess whether the agentic S2P narrative resonated in the 15-18 June presentations. AI positioning is a potential wedge (especially if competitors lack procurement-specific AI agents), but completely unvalidated.

Re-engage the procurement team urgently to surface the outcome of the 15-18 June vendor presentations, confirm whether Zycus is on the shortlist, and secure the next milestone (deeper demo, finalist selection, or commercial negotiation). The deal has been silent for 8-10 days post-presentation, which is a stall signal in a competitive RFI process where other vendors may be advancing while Zycus waits.

Map and engage the Economic Buyer (likely CFO Frances Yip, VP Finance Kanupriya Sharma, or Director FP&A Shameel Mehdi) to validate budget approval, pricing acceptance, and executive sponsorship. Without EB engagement, the $250k forecast is speculative and the deal is vulnerable to a late-stage budget or approval stall.

Identify and develop a champion within the procurement or finance function who has access to the Economic Buyer, understands the internal decision process, and is willing to advocate for Zycus against competitors. The current single-threaded engagement (zero contact roles, no named champion) is the highest risk factor for this deal going dark or being lost to a competitor without Zycus knowing.

gaps: Zero Avoma buyer calls matched after parallel opp+account discovery (15-char opp id 006P700000Wn0sT, 18-char account id queried). No transcripts, notes, or call metadata to extract MEDDPICC depth, competitive intel, stakeholder sentiment, or buyer-stated pain/ROI. This is a real knowledge gap that lowers confidence across all synthesized insight (Metrics, EB, Decision Criteria, Process, Pain, Champion, Competition).

Zero contact roles on the opportunity (OpportunityContactRole empty). No EB, DM, Champion, or Influencer roles assigned. The only named contact is Laetitia (from Next Step), with no title, role, or advocacy evidence. This is a knowledge gap (not hygiene) because the contacts genuinely are not mapped in Salesforce or visible on calls.

No MEDDPICC or competitor custom fields exist in this Salesforce org (INVALID_FIELD on all Q3 synthesis-source fields). The knowledge exists partially in Next Step field (scope, fitment, RFI timeline, pricing) but is not structured in named fields. This is a hygiene gap for field-fill metrics but a knowledge gap for MEDDPICC depth because the Next Step field carries process/scope, not quantified pain, EB identity, decision criteria weighting, or competitive names.

No AIS (AI excitement) fields exist in this org (INVALID_FIELD on AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c). Merlin is in product scope (ground-truth Products field), signaling AI appetite, but no AI positioning strength score, no buyer-stated AI requirements, and no validation of whether the agentic S2P narrative landed in presentations. This is a knowledge gap for AI fit assessment.

No stage-date submission fields exist in this org (INVALID_FIELD on Qualified_Submission_Date__c, Formal_Eval_Submission_Date__c, Shortlisted_Submission_Date__c). Time-in-stage and stage velocity cannot be computed from submission-date series; must rely on CreatedDate (15 April 2026) and infer at least 82 days in Formal Evaluation (or earlier stage not logged). This is a hygiene gap (field structure) but does not lower analysis confidence because the RFI timeline is documented in Next Step.

No Current_Contract_Expiration__c field exists in this org (INVALID_FIELD). No forcing function or incumbent replacement deadline visible to anchor urgency or paper-process timing. This is a knowledge gap for compelling event and timeline confidence.

No Next_Step_History__c field exists in this org (INVALID_FIELD). Deal pulse and progression narrative rely on Next_Step__c field (which IS populated and detailed) plus OpportunityFieldHistory (which has no StageName, Amount, or CloseDate changes logged beyond LastModifiedDate 16 June 2026). This is a hygiene gap (no history audit trail) but does not lower confidence because the Next Step field is rich.

No Tasks or Events logged post-18 June 2026 (presentation window). No buyer calls, emails, or meetings recorded after presentations ended. The 18-day silence is confirmed across Salesforce activity (LastActivityDate unknown/empty, no tasks/events) and Avoma (zero calls). This is a knowledge gap for post-presentation engagement and shortlist feedback.

No buyer feedback on RFI response (submitted 25 May), ROM pricing (presented 03 June), or vendor presentations (15-18 June) logged anywhere in Salesforce (Next Step, Tasks, Events, Description) or Avoma. This is a knowledge gap for competitive standing, pricing acceptance, and shortlist decision.

SC fitment assessment - I’ve completed an initial review of the Vancity requirement set and wanted to share a quick perspective from a fitment standpoint. The scope spans the full Source-to-Pay lifecycle, covering intake management strategic sourcing, supplier performance and risk management, contract lifecycle management, procurement and purchasing, invoicing, as well as reporting and analytics. Overall, this reflects a comprehensive, end-to-end S2P scope.

The only area that I saw as a snag would be automated redlining but this of course is quite a minor gap and can be solutioned. Based on this assessment, the alignment appears strong across the functional areas in scope, both in terms of capability coverage and overall solution positioning.
